title: silene pusilla subsp. albanica
intro: Silene pusilla subsp. albanica is a subspecies of flowering plant, also known as the small or alpine catchfly, belonging to the carnation family. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various infections. Silene pusilla subsp. albanica is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
